methodology: Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct (GQA) and SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct (MHA), WikiText-2, 1024 tokens. The shell tier (turbo_8bit/policy.turbo_config.bits) is exercised at multiple b values to measure quality vs compression. shared_frac=0.0625 isolates the shell as the dominant memory cost. Two-agent runs at shared_frac=0.5 and 0.95 measure the memory tradeoff between shared pool reuse and per-agent shell overhead.
key_findings: Shell tier is PPL-invariant to b on both Qwen0.5B and SmolLM2-1.7B for this corpus: b=2, 4, 8 all give delta_ppl_pct=+0.0000% bit-exact.
Shell storage is currently bloated by the JSON wire format (472B/block envelope). At long agent tails, this makes the hot tier LARGER than the raw bytes (0.23x reduction = 4.3x bloat).
When agent tails are short (26 tokens, shared_frac=0.95), the shell is small and the shared pool amortizes well: 4.97x memory reduction on N=2.
Implication: the hot tier needs the same compact wire format treatment that fib-quant got. Without it, multi-agent is a memory loss for long-tail agent scenarios.
